title: [DSP-7955 1/2] Implement cycle detection/resolution
description:
The following MR implements cycle detection via a Depth-First Search, recursively traversing the dependency graph until it detects that the next node would add a dependency that is already in the `IN_STACK` list, meaning that the DAG is no longer acyclic. When this occurs, a Slack/CLI notification is posted:

![image.png](/uploads/ccfec439b8f1fa4dfd163de809d3e82a/image.png){width=900 height=147}

This MR also implements an optional parameter, `fix_dag_cycle`, which is used when a cycle already exists in dynamo. In this case, even if the luna-dependencies.yaml has been updated, dynamodb can't be updated until seiji deploys. If Airflow can't deploy due to the cycle, there's no way to fix it in dynamo; thus: `fix_dag_cycle` allows Airflow to temporarily pop the cycled dependencies off of the DAG

Closes DSP-7955

diffs.diff:
@@ -396,6 +396,30 @@ class AirflowDeployTrigger:
 
         dag_id = f"{label}-{message_content['event_uuid'][-6:]}-{message_content['principal']}"
         dag_file, adt_event_file = self._generate_dag(message_content, dag_id)
+
+        cycle_error = self._preflight_cycle_check(dag_id)
+        if cycle_error:
+            self.logger.error(f"Dependency cycle detected for {dag_id}: {cycle_error}")
+            if self._str_to_bool(
+              self._get_ssm_parameter(f'/{self.label}/config/airflow_cd/slack_enabled')):
+                webhook = self._get_ssm_parameter(f'/{self.label}/config/airflow_cd/slack_webhook')
+                cycle_lines = self._parse_cycle_details(cycle_error)
+                if cycle_lines:
+                    formatted = "\n".join(f"• {c}" for c in cycle_lines)
+                    message = (
+                        f":x: *{self.label}*: DAG failed to register: dependency cycle detected"
+                        f"\n\n:recycle: *Detected cycles:*\n{formatted}"
+                    )
+                else:
+                    message = (
+                        f":x: *{self.label}*: DAG failed to register — dependency cycle detected"
+                        f"\n\n:recycle: `{cycle_error}`"
+                    )
+                self._send_notification(msg=message, webhook=webhook)
+            self._update_dynamodb_event_status(message_content['event_uuid'], 'DAG_CYCLE', details=cycle_error)
+            self._delete_message_and_cleanup(receipt_handle, message_id, dag_file, adt_event_file)
+            return
+
         self._run_airflow_cli_command("airflow dags reserialize", return_output=True)
 
         # Trigger the DAG
@@ -420,7 +444,38 @@ class AirflowDeployTrigger:
                 break
 
         if dag_retries >= dag_retry_max:
-            self.logger.error(f"DAG {dag_id} failed to trigger after {dag_retry_max} retries.")
+            import_error = self._get_dag_import_error(dag_id)
+            self.logger.error(f"DAG {dag_id} failed to trigger after {dag_retry_max} retries."
+                              f"{f' Import error: {import_error}' if import_error else ''}")
+
+            if self._str_to_bool(
+              self._get_ssm_parameter(f'/{self.label}/config/airflow_cd/slack_enabled')):
+                webhook = self._get_ssm_parameter(f'/{self.label}/config/airflow_cd/slack_webhook')
+                if import_error and 'cycle' in import_error.lower():
+                    cycle_lines = self._parse_cycle_details(import_error)
+                    if cycle_lines:
+                        formatted = "\n".join(f"• {c}" for c in cycle_lines)
+                        message = (
+                            f":x: *{self.label}*: DAG failed to register — dependency cycle detected"
+                            f"\n\n:recycle: *Detected cycles:*\n{formatted}"
+                            f"\n\n:wrench: Fix the `luna-dependencies.yaml` in the packages above to remove the circular reference."
+                        )
+                    else:
+                        message = (
+                            f":x: *{self.label}*: DAG failed to register — dependency cycle detected"
+                            f"\n\n:recycle: `{import_error}`"
+                            f"\n\n:wrench: Fix the `luna-dependencies.yaml` in the packages above to remove the circular reference."
+                        )
+                else:
+                    reason = f"\n\nImport error: `{import_error}`" if import_error else ""
+                    message = (
+                        f":x: *{self.label}*: DAG `{dag_id}` failed to register after {dag_retry_max} attempts"
+                        f"{reason}"
+                    )
+                self._send_notification(msg=message, webhook=webhook)
+
+            self._update_dynamodb_event_status(message_content['event_uuid'], 'FAILED')
+            self._delete_message_and_cleanup(receipt_handle, message_id, dag_file, adt_event_file)
             return
 
         dag_run_id = response.json().get('dag_run_id')
@@ -584,6 +639,46 @@ class AirflowDeployTrigger:
 
         return task_failures
 
+    def _preflight_cycle_check(self, dag_id):
+        try:
+            output = self._run_airflow_cli_command(
+                f"python /opt/airflow/dags/{dag_id}.py 2>&1",
+                return_output=True
+            )
+            if output and 'cycle' in output.lower():
+                for line in reversed(output.strip().split('\n')):
+                    if 'Cycles:' in line:
+                        return line.strip().lstrip('Exception: ').strip()
+        except Exception as e:
+            self.logger.warning(f"Pre-flight cycle check error: {e}")
+        return None
+
+    def _parse_cycle_details(self, import_error):
+        match = re.search(r'Cycles:\s*\[(.+?)\]', import_error)
+        if not match:
+            return None
+        raw = match.group(1)
+        cycles = [c.strip() for c in raw.split(';') if c.strip()]
+        return [f"`{c}`" for c in cycles]
+
+    def _get_dag_import_error(self, dag_id):
+        dag_filename = f"/opt/airflow/dags/{dag_id}.py"
+        for attempt in range(3):
+            try:
+                url = f"{self.airflow_webserver_url}/api/v1/importErrors"
+                response = requests.get(url, auth=self.auth_header)
+                if response.status_code == 200:
+                    for error in response.json().get('import_errors', []):
+                        if error.get('filename') == dag_filename:
+                            stack_trace = error.get('stack_trace', '')
+                            lines = [l for l in stack_trace.strip().split('\n') if l.strip()]
+                            return lines[-1] if lines else stack_trace
+            except Exception as e:
+                self.logger.warning(f"Failed to check DAG import errors: {e}")
+            if attempt < 2:
+                time.sleep(5)
+        return None
+
     def _get_manifest_for_tag(self, event_uuid):
         if self.region == "us-east-1":
             bucket_name = "abacus-artifacts-kitchen"
@@ -632,15 +727,18 @@ class AirflowDeployTrigger:
 
         return None  # Should be unreachable, but included for clarity
 
-    def _update_dynamodb_event_status(self, event_uuid, status):
+    def _update_dynamodb_event_status(self, event_uuid, status, details=None):
+        item = {
+            'event_uuid': {'S': event_uuid},
+            'status': {'S': status},
+            'timestamp': {'S': datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()},
+        }
+        if details:
+            item['details'] = {'S': details}
         try:
             self.dynamodb_client.put_item(
                 TableName=f"{self.label}-adt-event-state",
-                Item={
-                    'event_uuid': {'S': event_uuid},
-                    'status': {'S': status},
-                    'timestamp': {'S': datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()},
-                },
+                Item=item,
             )
             self.logger.info(f"Updated DynamoDB with status {status} for event {event_uuid}")

@@ -303,6 +303,84 @@ def get_secret_from_secrets_manager(secret_arn):
     return response['SecretString']
 
 
+def detect_dependency_cycles(tasks, components_split):
+    task_index = {task.task_id: idx for idx, task in enumerate(tasks)}
+
+    adj = [[] for _ in range(len(tasks))]
+    for idx, task in enumerate(tasks):
+        for downstream in task.downstream_list:
+            ds_idx = task_index.get(downstream.task_id)
+            if ds_idx is not None:
+                adj[idx].append(ds_idx)
+
+    UNVISITED, IN_STACK, DONE = 0, 1, 2
+    state = [UNVISITED] * len(tasks)
+    cycles = []
+
+    for start in range(len(tasks)):
+        if state[start] != UNVISITED:
+            continue
+        stack = [(start, iter(adj[start]))]
+        state[start] = IN_STACK
+        path = [start]
+
+        while stack:
+            node, neighbors = stack[-1]
+            try:
+                nxt = next(neighbors)
+                if state[nxt] == IN_STACK:
+                    cycle_start_pos = path.index(nxt)
+                    cycle = path[cycle_start_pos:] + [nxt]
+                    cycles.append(" -> ".join(components_split[i] for i in cycle))
+                elif state[nxt] == UNVISITED:
+                    state[nxt] = IN_STACK
+                    stack.append((nxt, iter(adj[nxt])))
+                    path.append(nxt)
+            except StopIteration:
+                state[node] = DONE
+                stack.pop()
+                path.pop()
+
+    return cycles
+
+
+def break_edge_cycles(edges, num_nodes, components_split):
+    adj = [[] for _ in range(num_nodes)]
+    for upstream, downstream in edges:
+        adj[upstream].append(downstream)
+
+    UNVISITED, IN_STACK, DONE = 0, 1, 2
+    state = [UNVISITED] * num_nodes
+    edges_to_remove = set()
+
+    for start in range(num_nodes):
+        if state[start] != UNVISITED:
+            continue
+        stack = [(start, iter(adj[start]))]
+        state[start] = IN_STACK
+        path = [start]
+
+        while stack:
+            node, neighbors = stack[-1]
+            try:
+                nxt = next(neighbors)
+                if state[nxt] == IN_STACK:
+                    edges_to_remove.add((node, nxt))
+                    logging.warning(
+                        f"fix_dag_cycle: removed edge {components_split[node]} -> {components_split[nxt]}"
+                    )
+                elif state[nxt] == UNVISITED:
+                    state[nxt] = IN_STACK
+                    stack.append((nxt, iter(adj[nxt])))
+                    path.append(nxt)
+            except StopIteration:
+                state[node] = DONE
+                stack.pop()
+                path.pop()
+
+    return [(u, d) for u, d in edges if (u, d) not in edges_to_remove]
+
+
 def get_exclusions(label):
     default_exclusions = set([exclusion.split(":")[0] for exclusion in CONFIG["exclusions"]])
     exclusions = default_exclusions.union(DAG_CONFIG["exclusions_json"])
@@ -456,6 +534,7 @@ with DAG(
                 task_id='predeploy-terraform-provider-task-empty'
             )
 
+    planned_edges = []
     for i, component in enumerate(components):
         coordinate = component.split(":")[0]
 
@@ -463,11 +542,11 @@ with DAG(
             descendant_artifacts = dependency_service.get_descendant_artifacts(coordinate=coordinate)
             for descendant in descendant_artifacts:
                 descendant_name = descendant.coordinate.split(":")[0]
-                if descendant_name not in exclusions and descendant_name in components_split:
+                if descendant_name not in exclusions and descendant_name in components_split and descendant_name != coordinate:
                     if OPERATION == 'destroy':
-                        tasks[components_split.index(descendant_name)].set_downstream(tasks[i])
+                        planned_edges.append((components_split.index(descendant_name), i))
                     else:
-                        tasks[i].set_downstream(tasks[components_split.index(descendant_name)])
+                        planned_edges.append((i, components_split.index(descendant_name)))
         elif OPERATION != 'destroy':
             try:
                 version = component.split(":")[1] if ":" in component else CONFIG['component_version']
@@ -478,6 +557,22 @@ with DAG(
                 )
                 for dep_coordinate in dependencies.keys():
                     if dep_coordinate in components_split:
-                        tasks[components_split.index(dep_coordinate)].set_downstream(tasks[i])
+                        planned_edges.append((components_split.index(dep_coordinate), i))
             except Exception as e:
                 logging.warning(f'Failed to get dependencies for new component {coordinate}: {e}')
+
+    if CONFIG.get('fix_dag_cycle', False):
+        planned_edges = break_edge_cycles(planned_edges, len(tasks), components_split)
+
+    for upstream_idx, downstream_idx in planned_edges:
+        tasks[upstream_idx].set_downstream(tasks[downstream_idx])
+
+    dependency_cycles = detect_dependency_cycles(tasks, components_split)
+    if dependency_cycles:
+        for cycle in dependency_cycles:
+            logging.error(f"Dependency cycle: {cycle}")
+        raise Exception(
+            f"Dependency cycle detected in DAG — cannot register. "
+            f"Cycles: [{'; '.join(dependency_cycles)}]. "
+            f"Fix the luna-dependencies.yaml in the packages above."
+        )

approvals.title: [DSP-7955 1/2] Implement cycle detection/resolution
approvals.description:
The following MR implements cycle detection via a Depth-First Search, recursively traversing the dependency graph until it detects that the next node would add a dependency that is already in the `IN_STACK` list, meaning that the DAG is no longer acyclic. When this occurs, a Slack/CLI notification is posted:

![image.png](/uploads/ccfec439b8f1fa4dfd163de809d3e82a/image.png){width=900 height=147}

This MR also implements an optional parameter, `fix_dag_cycle`, which is used when a cycle already exists in dynamo. In this case, even if the luna-dependencies.yaml has been updated, dynamodb can't be updated until seiji deploys. If Airflow can't deploy due to the cycle, there's no way to fix it in dynamo; thus: `fix_dag_cycle` allows Airflow to temporarily pop the cycled dependencies off of the DAG

Closes DSP-7955
